moral_set: Nature reveals itself.
Appearances are deceptive.
Appearances can be deceiving.
Self-help is the best help.
Look before you leap.
There's a time for work and a time for play.
He that is neither one thing nor the other has no friends.
He that has many friends, has no friends.
Flatterers are not to be trusted.
Do not trust flatterers.
Be content with your lot; one cannot be first in everything.
Be content with your lot.
To be satisfied with one's lot is better than to desire something which one is not fitted to receive.
It is not the place, nor the condition, but the mind alone that is responsible for our happiness.
One good turn deserves another.
An invitation prompted by selfishness is not to be accepted.
Every tale is not to be believed.
Be careful what you wish for.
Enemies' promises are made to be broken.
Union is strength.
Union gives strength.
Unity is strength, while division invites defeat.
Unity is mankind's greatest good, while dissension is a weak and slavish thing.
Nothing escapes the master's eye.
Someone who lays a trap for others will fall victim to it himself.
People who lay traps for others should be careful not to fall into a trap themselves.
People who lay traps for others bring about their own destruction.
Someone who plots against others falls into his own trap.
Those who scheme against others may ultimately fall victim to their own plots.
When people plot against their neighbours, they fall victim to the same sort of plot themselves.
People who do harm to others destroy themselves in the bargain.
People often make plans that turn out to be to their own disadvantage.
Bad neighbours are the first to suffer from the harm they would do to others.
Greed often overreaches itself.
Do not attempt too much at once.
Do not take on more than you can handle.
Don't take on more than you can accomplish.
A little thing in hand is worth more than a great thing in prospect.
Do not neglect small advantages in hopes of better ones.
Do not trade what might come, which is uncertain, for present opportunities, which can never be regained.
A bird in the hand is worth two in the bush.
Do not believe those who have ill intentions.
The word of a wicked man can never be trusted.
Never trust a known deceiver.
One should never trust those with a history of deceit.
People with a history of wrongdoing are often not trusted.
There is danger lurking in the words of a wicked person.
Do not ever trust your enemy. Always protect yourself from him, even if he comes to you humble and supplicating.
We should never put our trust in a wicked man, even if he seems to be completely innocuous.
Don't be seduced by deceptive words, they will cause nothing but trouble.
Beware of swindlers, as they often exploit the gullibility of others.
Believing your enemies makes you fall victim to their tricks and gets you into trouble.
The greatest kindness will not bind the ungrateful.
No gratitude from the wicked.
Expect no reward for serving the wicked.
Gratitude should be shown through kindness, not harm.
Try to please all and you end by pleasing none.
Those who seek to please everybody please nobody.
You can't please everybody.
Beware of when the powerful and the deceitful collaborate.
Wit always has an answer ready.
Be yourself and don't pretend to be someone you're not.
Do not be what you are not, nor pretend to be what you are not.
Do not try to gain favor by acting in a way that is contrary to your own nature and character.
Don't deceive yourself.
Men often revert to their natural instincts.
Change of habit cannot alter nature.
Some things you cannot change.
Changing your location does not change your state of mind.
Some people just won't change.
There is always someone worse off than yourself.
There is always someone else worse off than you.
Everyone is more or less master of his own fate.
Do not mock the gods.
True friends are hard to find.
True friends are hard to come by.
A life of greed and hoarding wealth leads to misery.
Indulgence in luxuries can lead to peril.
He laughs best that laughs last.
There is no wealth greater than liberty.
There is nothing more valuable than liberty.
Old friends should not be sacrificed for new ones.
Do not neglect the loyalty of old friends to please new acquaintances.
Do not give up friends for foes.
Don't take your friends for foes.
One is known by deeds, not by words.
Pay attention to deeds, not words.
People are to be judged by their actions, not by their appearance.
True worth should be measured by character and deeds, not by outward appearances.
Don't judge character by appearance.
Those who adapt to the times will emerge unscathed.
Obscurity often brings safety.
Humble commonalty easily finds safety in obscurity.
Do unto others what you would have them do unto you.
What you have done to others will likewise be done to you, according to the same measure.
The things that a person does to others will happen to him in turn.
Vices are their own punishment.
Seek to harm and harm shall find you.
Evil wishes, like chickens, come home to roost.
People are quick to promise but reluctant to give.
People are often quick to make promises, but they do not really expect to have to fulfill them.
People often make extravagant promises which they are completely unable to carry out.
Don't promise big things if you can't deliver.
Don't promise more than you can deliver.
Destroy the seed of evil, or it will grow up to your ruin.
It is useless attacking the insensible.
Do not count your chickens before they are hatched.
Our mere anticipations of life outrun its realities.
Expectations can lead to disappointment when reality does not match our desires.
There is little risk in being poor, while being rich can get you into trouble.
A humble life free from danger is more valuable than a life of luxury filled with peril.
The life of the poor is often safer than the life of the rich.
A wicked nature does not produce a good character.
No piece of luck can conceal a depraved nature.
A change of place does not make you a saint.
There is no believing a liar, even when he speaks the truth.
Don't be greedy.
A liar deceives no one but himself.
Wicked liars generally get their just consequences.
When you lie, you often have to expand the lie.
When wicked people do not get the reward and thanks they expect, they immediately put a stop to their good works.
Gratitude is the sign of noble souls.
In quarreling about the shadow we often lose the substance.
Men often bear little grievances with less courage than they do large misfortunes.
Clumsy jesting is no joke.
An ounce of prevention is worth a pound of cure.
It's good to be able to laugh at yourself.
You will only injure yourself if you take notice of despicable enemies.
It is easy to propose impossible remedies.
Each member in his proper sphere must work for the common good.
Necessity knows no law.
Be on guard against men who can strike from a distance.
One man's pleasure may be another's pain.
Whatever you do, do with all your might.
Men often applaud an imitation and hiss the real thing.
He who is once deceived is doubly cautious.
Those who enter by the back stairs may expect to be shown out at the window.
He who covets what belongs to another, deservedly loses his own.
How often do we supply our enemies with the means of our own destruction.
If words suffice not, blows must follow.
To do the right thing at the right season is a great art.
It is foolish to expect help from someone who is naturally unkind.
Better one safe way than a hundred on which you cannot reckon.
Cunning often outwits itself.
There is as much malice in a wink as in a word.
Better no rule than cruel rule.
Necessity is our strongest weapon.
We often despise what is most useful to us.
Avoid a remedy that is worse than the disease.
Strife feeds on conflict.
If you allow men to use you for your own purposes, they will use you for theirs.
The hero is brave in deeds as well as words.
Abstain and enjoy.
The desire for imaginary benefits often involves the loss of present blessings.
Sorrow not over what is lost forever.
Youth's first duty is reverence to parents.
Try before you trust.
Small liberties can be large offenses.
You may share the labours of the great, but you will not share the spoil.
Straws show how the wind blows.
Yield to all and you will soon have nothing to yield.
The man who talks for both sides is not to be trusted by either.
One story is good, till another is told.
Greatness has its penalties.
Don't make a big fuss over nothing.
Be sure of your pedigree before you boast of it.
Stoop to conquer.
Trouble comes from the direction we least expect it.
Self-interest alone moves some men.
Vicious people cannot resist hurting others even when it is not in their own interests.
Regardless of our wishes, or even our intent, it is to our Nature alone that we will be faithful.
Fine work gets preserved and treasured.
Two blacks do not make a white.
A bribe in hand betrays mischief at heart.
Every man for himself.
Never put yourself in your enemy's clutches.
If men had all they wished, they would be often ruined.
The loiterer often blames delay on his more active friend.
He who incites to strife is worse than he who takes part in it.
Equals make the best friends.
The strong and the weak cannot keep company.
He who shares the danger ought to share the prize.
The least outlay is not always the greatest gain.
Do not say anything at any time that you would not say at all times.
We are made by our teachings in youth.
There is strength in restraint.
A discerning speaker can deftly turn the insults of his enemies into compliments.
Great achievements are often blocked by some small thing which prevents their realization.
Sometimes a small sacrifice results in a greater good.
Necessity is the mother of invention.
When people harbor a criminal they become his first victims without even realizing it.
Resting the mind is essential for clear thinking.
It is unwise to reward when one should punish.
Success leads many astray to their ruin.
People are asking for trouble if they make fun of someone who is better than they are.
Experience often provides clearer insight than empty predictions.
A mild disposition can put a stop to vicious behaviour.
A mother's nurturing is stronger than a stepmother's attentions.
Foolish people disregard important business in favour of frivolities.
The dishonest, if they act honestly, get no credit.
Enviable conditions often have a downside.
Do no overestimate your worth when your fate rests in the hands of the high and the mighty.
Burdens can continue in unexpected ways.
People get themselves into trouble because of their boasting.
Prepare for the future.
Work honestly and you won't be scorned.
True value comes from usefulness.
He who does not conform to courtesy, mostly pays the penalty of his conceited behaviour.
If people who have previously committed a crime are swiftly punished, then others will become afraid on their account.
Everyone thinks themselves the best.
Do not take credit for the accomplishments of others.
Those who refuse to share and respond with rudeness may face dire consequences for their actions.
The true leader proves himself by his qualities.
A single reliable skill is worth more than a bag of useless tricks.
Be true to your roots.
You should think twice before offering anything to your enemies.
People are often eager to treat their enemies well in the hopes of gaining something by it.
Self-effort is essential.
Be frank and open in your dealings.
Some men underrate their best blessings.
Honor your craft with integrity.
We should not call upon the gods in trivial affairs but only in dire necessity.
Talking is a waste of time when you can simply provide a demonstration.
Do not let fame get to your head.
Seize opportunities when they arise.
Good things happen less often.
Intent matters more than the outcome.
Precautions are useless after the crisis.
Do not always stick to the same course.
Learn from past misfortunes.
Hunger sharpens the wits.
Sometimes one man toils while another profits.
What is valued in one group may be dismissed by another.
Beware of empty promises made during good times.
Disaster awaits the arrogant person who puts on airs: he will fail to get promoted and will lose his former position as well.
A learned man has always a fund of riches in himself.
It is easy to attack those who have fallen from their position of power and glory.
Self-preservation can sometimes take precedence over kinship.
Men feel worst when they contribute to their own undoing.
There is no glory in tricking those easy to trick.
Rushing into pleasant invitations can lead to dire consequences.
Inattentive people quickly notice anything that they think will benefit them, while they are completely unaware of things which are not their immediate concern.
Do not laugh at the calamity that befalls your neighbour.
Evil tendencies are shown in early life.
Rewards do not always accompany a lifetime of good work.
Only cowards insult dying majesty.
It is good to set aside our deadly disputes and conflicts, since they put everyone's lives in jeopardy.
Counsel without help is useless.
Anything which is done at the wrong time is liable to be ridiculed.
An honest man's word is as good as his oath.
Some men are of more consequence in their own eyes than in the eyes of their neighbors.
People who enter into contests with unworthy opponents lower their own reputation.
When you are besieged by powerful people, you are subject to vicious attacks by anyone and everyone.
Those who take by force are met with resistance, while those who approach with humility may find unexpected generosity.
It is best to keep negative influences away.
Experience teaches valuable lessons and encourages caution.
Jealousy can lead people to foolishly imitate their betters.
Be cautious with promises made under duress.
Stay focused on your goals.
Gossips are to be seen and not heard.
Relying too much on subordinates can lead to unintended consequences.
One bad turn deserves another.
When the evidence is fairly weighed, a colourfully painted lie is quickly refuted by the facts.
Opposites are utterly incompatible.
Do not go chasing after things that are useless and unprofitable.
The penalty for speaking and for keeping silent is one and the same.
Men will stand behind a judgment made in error until the actual fact of the matter later compels them to regret their choice.
It is natural to be a knave; men must rise above.
Only fools indulge into michief and excessive risks.
An alliance made with the high and mighty can never be trusted.
Wealth is of little value if one is too afraid to use or enjoy it.
True courage involves overcoming fears, even when faced with intimidation.
The useful is of much more importance and value, than the ornamental.
You should not treat bad people the same way that you treat good people.
If you try to deceive someone who has his wits about him, you will waste your time and be made fun of as well.
Those who commit grave wrongs cannot escape justice.
Live your lives in the clothes that suit you.
Vanity betrays every fraud.
Do not boast to have something you do not.
Many children are a great blessing; but a few good ones are a greater.
Tricksters can outsmart the strong.
Bad things naturally happen to bad people, while good things lie in wait for people who are honest and reasonable.
Good will is worth nothing unless it is accompanied by good acts.
Do not harm your benefactors.
Watch on all sides.
When it serves their purposes, people commonly do not hesitate to swear to the most incredible fabrications.
Someone who assists both the truly good and those who are undeserving is not seen as a philanthropist, but is instead regarded as a madman.
A death-bed flattery is the worst of treacheries.
Few things are done except for profit.
Do not play the wise man after the fact.
Different people are interested in different things.
False sympathy often masks selfish intentions.
You should especially avoid someone who does not even spare his own people.
Trust is not to be given lightly.
Those who try to deceive may expect to be paid in their own coin.
Men are averse to fixed rules, but are won by kind services.
Often justice is the captive of criminal deceit.
Enterprise is not to be judged in terms of its speed but its outcome.
Take what you can get when you can get it.
Do not take pride in the good qualities that actually belong to someone else.
Benefits are refused by those who don't understand them.
No one can be a friend if you know not whether to trust or distrust him.
People who offer to give someone many gifts are no doubt trying to deceive.
Do not blame others for the circumstances of their upbringing.
There is satisfaction, however wretched, in taking one's enemy along if one has to die.
Some can do wrong more easily than others can see it.
Traitors may expect treachery.
Be cautious of pride, as nobody knows what the future may bring.
The loud-mouthed boaster does not impress nor frighten those who know him.
Talent is wasted when it's in the hands of the unappreciative.
Instead of looking at how things start, we should look instead at how they turn out in the end.
People often appreciate what they had only after experiencing something worse.
The insubordinate are free from both obligation and protection.
In a change of government, the poor change nothing beyond the name of their master.
Easy life and seeming good fortune of others conceal a threat to their welfare.
One cannot circumvent his destiny.
You can't always stop a fool.
It's hard to give up bad habits.
Beauty alone does not qualify one for leadership.
God is the ultimate judge.
Some people will do anything to get what they want.
Do not boast too much at the height of your powers; people often spend their old age worn out with toil and trouble.
The only thing that can really bring shame on a man is a punishment that he has justly deserved.
Charity begins at home, but this does not prevent the further exercise of it.
Those who complain about their duties often forget their own role in creating the situation.
He who harms someone should henceforth be treated with suspicion.
Injuries may be forgiven, but not forgotten.
Man's greatest treasure consists in work.
We had better bear our troubles bravely than try to escape them.
Imitation is no substitute for knowledge.
We have to endure reversals of fortune, since we know that life is a matter of luck.
Luck often bestows the things that skill cannot obtain.
Some never understand the actions of others.
A reward for one might be destruction for another.
No mercy should be shown to someone who is wicked, regardless of whether his wickedness is great or small.
Pleasure bought with pains, hurts.
Ignore complainers who have no influence.
Coffers of cash put a stop to honest traffic in praise.
A wicked person deservedly comes to a bad end by keeping company with other wicked people.
It's easy to despise what you cannot have.
Overly curious people often pay a very high price for recklessly indulging their curiosity.
Ambiguity in character can lead to mistrust.
Chance sometimes rules.
Deceivers often exploit the inability of others to challenge their falsehoods.
Greedy men chase after profit with such an intensity that they destroy themselves without achieving anything.
Take pleasure when thieves destroy themselves.
Distrust advice from someone who stands to gain.
Beware of those who appear busy but achieve little of value.
People who stubbornly cling to unwise choices lose everything before they know it.
Poor folk suffer when the high and mighty are at war with one another.
When you need someone to lend a hand, mere words are no help at all.
Easy gains often come with hidden costs and hardships.
Bear that those who are inferior to you in merit should be like you in outside appearances.
Beware of enterprizes where the risks are not equal.
Do not expect to be defended by someone who cannot offer any protection.
Sometimes, the greatest threats come from those you trust.
A fool will make excuses for foolish behavior.
It is often the case that people are greedy for things which they think will bring them honour, but which only result in their death and destruction.
Vanity can blind one to their true worth in the eyes of others.
Physical stature does not guarantee wisdom or intelligence.
Honesty is the best policy.
The promises of the greedy are often empty and insincere.
When people suffer misfortune because of their own foolishness, they often blame the gods.
Life's disasters can often bring boastful and presumptuous people back down to earth.
There is no fixed day on which wicked people are punished.
Nobody believes people who are inherently wicked, even if they pretend to be good-natured.
Each man makes his reputation in the field which is his specialty.
Acceptance can lead to unexpected harmony.
People who have been judged in the past can later on be in a position to judge their former teachers.
Do not let anything turn you from your purpose.
Every man should be content to mind his own business.
The safeguards of virtue are hateful to those with evil intentions.
Caution is far superior to reckless haste and hurry.
People are willing to risk their lives for the sake of some petty profit.
When your adversary is also your judge, don't bother arguing.
Those who thrive on deception often retaliate against those who speak the truth.
Things you acquire through evil means can be taken from you by evil means.
You will hurt yourself and accomplish nothing if you try to imitate someone who is far better than you are.
The greatest gifts are often overlooked in favor of envying others.
Someone who cannot manage his own affairs is not qualified to give advice to his neighbours.
Marrying someone unworthy can lead to a tarnished reputation.
The true value of money is not in its possession but in its use.
Boast of one thing and you will be found lacking in that and a few other things as well.
A sinful mind can even change a person's nature, causing it to be impaired.
Those who avoid hardship may miss out on valuable rewards.
The best intentions will not always ensure success.
Tear out vice by the roots and the branches will wither away.
In conflicts between powerful parties, bystanders may suffer unintended consequences.
If often happens that even honest people do not hesitate to persecute someone who has slandered them.
There's no reading of ourselves but with other mans eyes.
When people feel grief for their misfortunes, they avoid the place where that grief overtook them.
Persuasion is better than Force.
The memory of a good deed lives.
People often disguise their selfish desires as practical needs to justify their actions.
Half-truths in some cases, are foul, and dishonest; in others, laudable; and in some again, not only honest, but necessary.
People who have been treated with contempt repay the deed in kind.
A wise person readily tolerates the insolence of strangers when he sees those same strangers mistreating one another.
Temptation can lead even the most virtuous individuals to compromise their integrity and values.
The bad things that strangers do to you are never as terrible as the things done to you by the members of your own family.
Some people will agree to do things under compulsion that they are not willing to do as a favour.
No one gladly revisits a place where they got hurt.
The unjust man is envious of anyone else's good fortune.
Desire things you don't understand and you accept the consequences.
Sometimes, those you support become your worst enemy.
Not all advice is wise.
Do not blame others.
That is how life is: we must put up with less serious losses in order to avoid worse ones.
Modesty and carnal love cannot survive together.
Appetite blinds men to their surroundings.
Some may choose mutual destruction over continued suffering.
Even the favours of wicked people are frightening.
Those who neglect their own affairs while meddling in others' lives often reveal their own shortcomings.
What is most truly valuable is often underrated.
People often prefer to live simply at home than to live lavishly at someone else's house.
Those who act in their own self-interest while pretending to help others will not earn gratitude.
Bad situations rarely get better.
Even those who work hard may find their material wealth undone by the greed of others.
Do not overestimate yourself.
Do not believe everything you hear.
You can fool someone only so many times.
Telling the truth can sometimes be dangerous.
You should not add one problem to another.
Try to make something, and do not let envy pass judgment on it. Nothing is entirely satisfactory to the dishonest.
People adjust their beliefs based on what is profitable for them.
Unresolved issues can resurface when least expected.
Fortune can occasionally bestow favours that exceed a person's hopes and expectations.
Only a man who is out of his mind and hated by the gods delights in ugly things as if they were beautiful.
It is difficult to truly know a person.
Don't draw a conclusion based on a single observation.
Beauty is in the eyes of the beholder.
Notoriety is often mistaken for fame.
They complain most who suffer least.
Do not be in a hurry to change one evil for another.
Some would rather fight than not.
Use care to place your blame on the right person.
Beware the sort of person who uses tricks.
A greedy man does not gladly give anything away, even when he has enough for himself.
Someone who wants to help his friend must give him what is essential and appropriate.
An enemy is most dangerous when offering you help.
The things which bring us pleasure can often be hazardous as well.
No one should boast when his prowess is a matter of mere circumstance.
No curse weighs more heavily than a guilty conscience.
Love can blind even the wildest.
Do not attempt to hide things which cannot be hid.
Example is more powerful than precept.
A coward by nature will always be a coward.
The man who treats others well is rewarded by gratitude.
A man cannot be too careful of what he does, where the life of any creature is in question.
Trouble awaits you if you pursue ill-gotten gains.
The person who turns to a dishonest scoundrel for help in times of trouble will be ruined, not rescued.
It is dangerous to believe a story, and dangerous not to believe it.
Often there is more good to be found in one man than in a crowd of people.
If you don't speak out for others, there will be nobody left to speak out for you.
Tears don't always signify pity.
People with excellent qualities are often insulted by their inferiors.
A bad man can come to ruin, but he is able to harm many other people first.
It is better to be injured with impunity, than to put ourselves in the power of another.
Choosing unworthy representatives can lead to humiliation and ongoing disrespect for the entire group.
Life is a mixture of grief and joy.
Avoid the gain that exposes to hazard.
We must not require what is unreasonable.
The essence of authenticity cannot be replicated.
The meaning is to be considered, not the mere words.
Don't go to extremes.
The weak may find a way to avenge a wrong.
Take care of the little things and the big things will take care of themselves.
Avoid overindulgence.
Wit outshines skill.
Each stage of life has its own gifts.
When a man has both an interest and an inclination to betray us, there's no trusting him.
In times of peril, the truth may be bitter, but it can also be a means of escape.
Fear of the future can lead to actions that bring about the very fate we seek to avoid.
We must help one another, for if a neighbor falls, their burden will fall on you.
The grass is always greener on the other side of the fence.
Professional mourners serve those who pay the most.
Justice is fickle.
Honor the customs of the household you enter.
Do not let your fancy make you forget realities.
Look at the source when placing the blame.
It is better to be loved than feared.
Foresake the hard when an easier way is available.
Even gods may not know everything.
Act as you profess to believe.
Respect is often better shown by refraining from disrespect.
Sometimes, Wicked people commit crime in haste but are not punished until much later.
Care defines ability.
Know yourself and your limits.
Trust but verify.
The great do not always prevail.
Falsehood is with all men.
Consider all before you judge.
People that live by example need to look closely at the example they are following.
Know your place.
Be watchful or all will be lost.
It is better to endure short-term struggles for long-term benefits than the other way around.
When fools fight others run away with the prize.
There is no easy choice during desperate times.
Do not dwell on what has passed.
Sadness is a part of life.
Action is more valuable than arguments.
It's easy to put off today what can be done tomorrow.
Be prepared to defend your position.
Degrading your opponents only diminishes your own victory.
Value is what others think of you, not what you think of yourself.
There is danger in the silent enemy.
We should not love our homeland so much that we ignore the possibility of finding greater happiness elsewhere.
A small inconvenience is compensated for by a greater usefulness.
A wise person will not attempt anything without having the right means to achieve it.
Trusting as you wish to be trusted doesn't work between a rogue and an honest person.
We should not to conclude that we are allowed to do to other people's things what is permitted to them in their own houses.
What we are forced to do by a greater power and necessity is not truly our own action.
Famous beauties are rarely lucky.
Do not fear death, as it is the final goodbye to all the troubles, pains, and dangers of life.
Beware those who value their gain more than their love.
Arrogant fools have no true influence.
In all disputes, those who keep their opponents afraid of revenge come out best.
Men would bear their misfortunes more patiently if they considered the ones of others.
The shortest and surest way of doing things is best.
An alliance with the powerless is futile.
There is a clever way of joking between seriousness and jest that brings both enjoyment and benefit.
Honors change behavior.
Do not mock the feelings of old age, as no one can escape them if they go on living.
All things are to be done in their own time.
Big talkers are not suited for trust, business, or conversation.
Putting up with one insult invites another.
Only untrustworthy people blame the consequences of their own actions on others.
Embrace your own strengths.
Consider the consequences of your actions.
What a person does through another is truly their own action.
It's better to ignore an insult from one troublemaker than to bring a whole crowd of trouble upon yourself.
Everything pleases at first, but nothing pleases for long.
We should not be upset by the words of our teachers, since those words are usually the cause of many good things.
It's wise and kind to be considerate of one another, for no one knows their own end.
Nothing should be condemned, since there is nothing so worthless and humble that it cannot at some time or another prove to be useful.
It is more honorable to die having achieved noble deeds than to live a life of vice.
Unequal matches bring misery.
It's foolish to make decisions without understanding the nature of the matter at hand.
Everyone should be satisfied with their own lot in life, given that disaster awaits us on every side.
When public officials encourage foolishness, it's no surprise if fools try to become public officials.
He who claims any good for himself takes away from the source of all the good he enjoys.
We should not expect that our crimes will always go unpunished.
It is better by far to be governed by one chief, than by many.
No one is so loving of a friend that they would not prefer their own good to that of another.
Not all things are suitable for all people.
Even beneficial things all appear to be drastic and sure to hurt for those those who are unfamiliar with them and inexperienced.
Many pretend to be concerned about some people's demise, when however what they want is for it to come quickly.
Every Season has its own work.
Save a Thief from the Gallows, and he'll cut your Throat.
Unwanted visits are a nuisance to a peaceful person's life.
Possession is nine-tenths of the law.
Better to be alone than in bad company.
There is no thing so worthy of wonderment which does not cease to be a miracle with the passage of time.
Men who are wickedly inclined will not stop their vicious behavior, no matter who urges them to do so.
When people won't listen to reason through kind means, they must be made to understand through harsh ones.
Complaining is the privilege of the weak.
Money opens all doors.
Even the strongest can be brought low by unchecked desires.
Those who won't learn from advice must be taught by experience.
Educate your children in those arts which are capable of preserving them from want.
No one is so contemptible that he cannot be of use to us in some way.
The conflicts of the wicked are the protection of the good.
We should live as if we always saw Death standing by.
Contentment with our lot is an element of happiness.
Quality is better than quantity.
Quality is more valuable than quantity.
Do not attempt the impossible.
Do not try to do impossible things.
You must not act unnaturally, trying to achieve some impossible thing.
Fine clothes may disguise, but silly words will disclose a fool.
Clothes may disguise a fool, but his words will give him away.
A fine coat is not always an indication of an attractive mind.
No act of kindness, no matter how small, is ever wasted.
Kindness is never wasted.
In avoiding a lesser danger, one may inadvertently fall into a greater peril.
When we avoid present dangers, we must weigh the degree of danger of the alternatives
Better to endure a small hardship than risk a greater one.
Better to face a known danger than to trust a deceitful promise of safety.
False confidence often leads into danger.
Overconfidence often leads people into danger.
People often grudge others what they cannot enjoy themselves.
Some begrudge others what they cannot enjoy themselves.
Some people are so consumed by hatred that they would rather suffer themselves than see their enemies succeed.
To be small is a way to stay safe and avoid problems, whereas you rarely see a man with a big reputation who is able to keep out of danger.
Better poverty without care, than riches with.
It is better to live in self-sufficient poverty than to be tormented by the worries of wealth.
It is better to serve in safety than to rule in peril.
People often grasp for more than they need and thus lose the little they have.
People who grasp for more than they need are deprived of what they have.
Those who are not content with what they have may end up losing it while pursuing greater desires.
Greed for more can lead to losing what you already have.
People who grasp at more than they need lose the little that they held in their hands.
Do not grasp at more than you need.
Do not try to grasp too much at once.
Do not ask for more than you deserve.
We should do favours for someone who can do us a good deed in return.
Favor those who do you kindness.
Those who do you a favour you must repay kind.
Return favors and favors will be bestowed on you.
Stay vigilant and think before you speak.
It's wise to think before we speak.
Familiarity mollifies even the most terrifying things.
Familiarity makes it easy to confront even frightening situations.
Familiarity can alleviate seemingly intractable problems.
Do not fight with those who are perfectly capable of fighting back.
We should not pick fights with people stronger than we are.
Know your enemies.
Pick your enemies.
Watch the actions of your enemy.
It is a foolish man who wants to rival his superiors.
He who competes with his superiors destroys himself before he can equal them.
Preparedness for war is the best guarantee of peace.
No one can be safe in peace unless they are always ready to face an enemy in case of war.
Many people renounce wicked activities only because they are prevented from conducting them any longer.
Many people give up their vices not out of a love for virtue, but because they can no longer continue indulging in them.
Even a high and mighty person should beware of his inferiors; their ingenuity can find a way to take revenge.
It is very unwise, even for the greatest of men, to provoke the least, as they may find a way to enact revenge.
An enemy in disguise is much more unforgivable than an open enemy.
A false friend is the worst kind of enemy.
A man is known by the company he keeps.
You are judged by the company you keep.
Birds of a feather flock together.
Happy is the man who learns from the misfortunes of others.
A discerning person is made wise by the misfortunes of his neighbours.
Take warning from the misfortunes of others.
Little by little does the trick.
Slow and steady wins the race.
Those who pretend to be what they are not, sooner or later, find themselves in deep water.
Pretenders will be found out.
Those who assume a character which does not belong to them, only make themselves ridiculous.
A hypocrite can usually be found out.
Hypocritical speeches are easily seen through.
Nature and instincts can persist despite changes in form or appearance.
When someone with a wicked nature changes his appearance, his behaviour remains the same.
Tyrants need no excuse.
Any excuse will serve a tyrant.
Misfortune tests the sincerity of friends.
Fair weather friends are not worth much.
When we form friendships with one another, we must choose allies who can come to our aid in moments of peril.
We must make friends in prosperity if we would have their help in adversity.
Seek the friendship of those who do not withdraw from offering help even in a time of adversity.
Brag and nature may bring you down.
Vanity can lead to self-destruction.
Pride goes before destruction.
No matter how you brag, you can be undone.
Do not boast lest you be taken down.
Pride and vanity leads to our downfall.
Precious things are for those that can prize them.
True value is wasted on those who cannot appreciate it.
It is not only fine feathers that make fine birds.
Inner beauty is better than outer beauty.
Outside show is a poor substitute for inner worth.
Those who would mend others, should first mend themselves.
It is a foolish thing to give advice to others while not looking out for oneself.
Men often condemn others for what they see no wrong in doing themselves.
We are not able to see our own faults: but as soon as others make a slip, we are ready to censure.
Consider carefully before changing your situation.
Understand what you are doing before you do it.
Count the cost before you commit yourselves.
They who act without sufficient thought, will often fall into unsuspected danger.
People who act impulsively can end up in regrettable situations.
Accept the changes in life.
Whatever happens, we must bear it with equanimity.
Where force is not enough, cleverness must be used instead.
Wisdom is far superior to strength.
Courage in words means nothing if you flee in danger.
It is easy to be brave when there is no danger.
Imaginary fears are the worst.
Many people fear danger where there is none, and feel safe where there is danger: put things in perspective.
